data_source: FEC.gov, public polling, Polymarket, Kalshi, press reports
observations: Platner raised $7.87M through Dec 31, 2025 — overwhelming small-dollar grassroots: 99% of Q4 donations under $200 (avg $25.88).
Collins has cash-on-hand advantage ($8.0M vs Platner's $3.7M) but faces structural headwinds with -30 net unfavorable among independents.
Senate Leadership Fund committed ~$42M in outside spending to defend Collins — the biggest outside spending commitment in the race.
Platner leading every public primary poll: 55-28 (Emerson, Mar 2026), 64-26 (UNH, Feb 2026), 66-28 (Impact Research, Mar 2026).
Prediction markets heavily favor Platner: 92% on Polymarket, 80-82% on Kalshi to win Democratic primary.
General election polling shows Platner ahead of Collins: 48-41 (Emerson), 49-38 (UNH), 44-40 (Pan Atlantic).
Platner campaign pivoting to general election focus against Collins as of April 2026.
Instagram is Platner's dominant social channel (~310K followers), dwarfing X (~84.8K) and Facebook (~118K).
Campaign launch video received an estimated 2.5M views in 24 hours — unprecedented for a first-time Senate candidate.
Platner raised $1M in first 9 days (avg donation $33) and $4.6M in Q4 2025 alone — one of the strongest small-dollar Senate fundraising runs in recent cycles.

primary.note: Average across Emerson (55/28), UNH (64/26), Pan Atlantic (46/39), Impact Research (66/28)

general.note: Average across Emerson (48/41), UNH (49/38), Pan Atlantic (44/40)
